session_label: 2023 Digital Orientalist article cross-check: Persian manuscript resources
scope: User-prompted coverage check: enumerate every digital resource catalogued in https://digitalorientalist.com/2023/02/10/digital-resources-for-the-study-of-persian-manuscripts/ and verify whether each was probed in the v1.0.0 deposit (search_2026-05-02_*.json sessions and probe_2026-05-10_iran_caucasus_reachability.json) or in the 2026-05-10/11 follow-on rounds. Where coverage gaps surface, run a Cihanşah / Hakîkî / Yusuf Hakîkî probe against the resource and document the outcome. Goal: a defensible answer to 'have we used all the relevant resources from the article'.
geopolitical_caveat: Iranian internet infrastructure is currently subject to wartime intermittent shutdowns (2026 conflict). Iranian-portal status documented here is point-in-time as of 2026-05-11. Western-library status is not affected by the geopolitical caveat.

method_summary.tier_1_inventory_extraction: WebFetch against the 2023 Digital Orientalist article to extract a complete list of every resource named, with verbatim URL, type (manuscript catalogue / digital images / IIIF / Telegram / aggregator / printed catalogue), institution, and access notes.
method_summary.tier_2_prior_coverage_check: Grep against existing search-session JSONs (search_2026-05-02_iran_caucasus, _uk_european, _south_asian_global, _turkey_anatolia, _auctions, _tezkire, _mecmua_nesimi_adjacency, _catalogue_scribe_edition_backchain, plus probe_2026-05-10_iran_caucasus_reachability and search_2026-05-10_marashi_tehran_followup) to determine which of the 28 article resources had already been probed in v1.0.0 work.
method_summary.tier_3_gap_probing: For resources not previously probed, direct probe via WebFetch with Cihanşah / Jahanshah / Hakîkî / Yusuf Hakîkî query; if WebFetch returns 403/UA-block or JS-rendered content, escalate to Playwright; if rate-limited or returns Anubis-style anti-bot challenge, document as residual gap and defer.
method_summary.tier_4_search_endpoint_verification: For resources where the article URL points to a browse page rather than a search endpoint, identify the actual query URL pattern and run the Cihanşah query battery.

resource_crosscheck.name: Melli Library Historical Documents (asnad.org)
resource_crosscheck.type: Digital images of historical Persian documents
resource_crosscheck.institution: Chair of Iranian Studies, University of Bamberg, Germany (NOT NLAI directly — the article appears to misattribute; asnad.org is a German DFG-funded project that aggregates Persian historical documents from various sources including but not limited to NLAI)
resource_crosscheck.prior_coverage: NOT PROBED in v1.0.0 deposit. This is a coverage gap.
resource_crosscheck.this_session_action: FULL PROBE: site reachable HTTP 200; search box submitted with Persian-script Cihanşah / Karakoyunlu queries via Playwright; multiple result pages snapshotted

queries_executed.outcome: phrase-exact match returns zero — search uses literal phrase matching
MAJOR HIT — at least 9 documents directly attributed to Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu visible on first page (firmans, soyurghal, kitabe inscription, letter); see chancery_documents_surfaced field below
Latin-script search returns 21 (slight delta from Persian-script 23 — likely due to script-variant matching); confirmed 9 chancery documents + Yusuf Mirza b. Jahanshah vaqf-namah (1490, posthumous)
1926/1344 marriage contract for Mirza 'Ali Aqa Haqiqi — Qajar-era namesake, NOT 15th-century Cihanşah Hakîkî
zero — Latin-script Karakoyunlu form not used; database uses Persian قره قویونلو / قراقویونلو variants

chancery_documents_surfaced.title_en: Farman by Khatun Jan Begum, spouse of Jahanshah Qara Quyunlu
Letter from Jahanshah b. Qara Yusuf
Vaqf-namah by Yusuf Mirza b. Jahanshah Qara Quyunlu

resource_crosscheck.verdict: MAJOR FINDING — 21-23 Cihanşah-related chancery / inscription / correspondence documents surfaced. ZERO poetry-related documents (no tezkires, no divans). Out of v1.0 poetry-only Dīvān witness register scope. CRITICAL for v2.0 chancery / inscription / correspondence scope: this is likely the largest single source for v2.0 chancery materials currently identified. URL pattern for individual documents: /en/document/<id>/ — usable by future v2.0 retrieval.

resource_crosscheck.name: DLME (Digital Library of the Middle East)
resource_crosscheck.institution: Digital Library of the Middle East consortium
resource_crosscheck.prior_coverage: Probed in search_2026-05-02_south_asian_global.json — 'No public query endpoint surfaced + 404 on dlme-review/api/search'; listed as residual gap
resource_crosscheck.this_session_action: RE-PROBE: WebFetch against the article's specific browse URL — returned 575 Persian manuscripts, contributing institutions Bodleian / U Michigan / Library of Congress / Leiden; search interface fields visible (Everything / Title / Creator / Identifier); URL pattern /library/catalog/<UUID>; tried direct query endpoint /library/search?q=Jahanshah+Hakiki+Qaraqoyunlu — 404 Not Found (search endpoint URL still not surfaced this round)
resource_crosscheck.verdict: PARTIAL — 575 Persian manuscripts indexed by aggregator but no working query-by-text endpoint surfaced from outside the JS interface. Manual browse of 575 records would be needed to definitively rule out a Cihanşah witness; deferred. Note: the contributing institutions (Bodleian, Michigan, LoC, Leiden) are likely already covered by Fihrist (UK) which we exhaustively probed in v1.0.

residual_gaps_breakdown.Chester Beatty viewer.cbl.ie: Anubis bot challenge; headed browser would resolve

summary.v1_register_impact: ZERO new poetry witnesses. The v1.0 deposit's 14-witness register is unaffected. asnad.org's 21-23 Cihanşah hits are all chancery documents, inscriptions, correspondence — out of v1.0 scope (poetry-only Dīvān).
summary.v2_register_impact: MAJOR — asnad.org alone provides ~9 directly-attributed Cihanşah chancery documents (firmans, soyurghal, kitabe inscription, letter) spanning 1434-1467, plus 1 posthumous vaqf-namah by his son Yusuf Mirza. This is likely the largest single source for v2.0 chancery-document scope. Combined with already-known Cihanşah inscriptions (Kabud Mosque Tabriz, etc.) and coinage (numismatic catalogues), the v2.0 expansion has substantially better source-corpus coverage than the Section 1 design assumed.
key_methodological_findings: Cross-checking against the 2023 Digital Orientalist article surfaced 7 resources not previously probed in v1.0; one of these (asnad.org) yielded a major finding that fundamentally changes the v2.0 source-corpus picture.

The article's misattribution of asnad.org as 'Melli Library Historical Documents' (NLAI) is misleading — asnad.org is actually a German DFG-funded project at the University of Bamberg, NOT an NLAI subsidiary. This explains why it was NOT geo-IP blocked when other Iranian portals were.

Of the 6 residual gaps, 4 are JS-rendered SPAs (Vienna ONB, Berlin, OPenn, MENA-DOC) that would require Playwright DOM rendering rather than WebFetch HTML extraction; 1 is rate-limited (Bologna 1-hour Retry-After); 1 is anti-bot-protected (CBL Anubis).

Of the 4 already-probed Western Iranian poetry-witness aggregators (Fihrist UK, Cambridge cudl, Met Cochran, BnF Gallica), Fihrist is the most exhaustively covered — and has been treated as the witness-discovery source-of-truth for v1.0.

The 21-23 asnad.org chancery hits, plus the 9 confirmed Cihanşah-attributed firmans/soyurghals/inscriptions/letters, represent a v2.0-only finding — they do not interact with the v1.0 poetry witness count or the original BL Or 9493 decoration question (which remains triangulated NEGATIVE).
summary.register_changes: none — witness_register.json is unchanged by this session; the 14-entry roster persists
follow_on_targets_for_future_session: v2.0 design: expand the source-corpus matrix to include asnad.org as the primary chancery-document source. Document the URL pattern /en/document/<id>/ and the per-document fields (date_hijri / date_western / sender / recipient / type / location).
v2.0 retrieval: pull all 21-23 asnad.org Cihanşah hits via /en/document/<id>/ for each result; capture full document images, transcriptions, and any prosopographic data.

Residual-gap closure (lower priority): Playwright passes against Vienna ONB, Berlin Staatsbibliothek, Halle MENA-DOC; rate-limit-respecting retry of Bologna historica.unibo.it; headed-browser pass at Chester Beatty viewer.cbl.ie. Each is low expected yield given Fihrist exhaustiveness.

If v1.0.1 or later patch is published: include this cross-check artifact + the companion probe_2026-05-11_institutional_catalogue_rediscovery.json + an update to README documenting the asnad.org finding as a v2.0-relevant discovery (without changing the v1.0 register).
